case_title: HARRY PAGE VS. SAID AHMED MOHSIN

docket_entries.description: SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF DEC-02-2008 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF DEC-02-2008 BY PAUL SLAVIT. APPEARANCES BY BOTH PARTIES. EXHIBITS RETAINED. CLERK: S. VANTREE. NOT REPORTED. (506).
APPLICATION FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68511.3 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PAGE, HARRY ORDER GRANTING WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CRC 3.50 - 3.63
SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF JAN-05-2009 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF JAN-05-2009 BY PAUL SLAVIT.APPEARANCE BY DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF NOT PRESENT. . EXHIBITS RETAINED. S. VANTREE. NOT REPORTED. (506).
MONEY CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $7,500.00 FILED BY PAGE, HARRY AS TO DEFENDANT MOHSIN, SAID AHMED DBA TENDERLOIN LIQUOR ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR DEC-02-2008 AT 03:30 PM IN DEPT 506
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED SERVED OCT-21-2008, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT MOHSIN, SAID AHMED DBA TENDERLOIN LIQUOR
DECLARATION OF APPEARANCE ON BEHALF OF CORPORATION PURSUANT TO CCP 116.540 (B)-(H) FILED BY DEFENDANT MOHSIN, SAID AHMED DBA TENDERLOIN LIQUOR

SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF DEC-02-2008 - CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JAN-05-2009 AT 3:30 PM IN DEPT. 506. THE COURT RETURNS THIS CASE TO CALENDAR SO THAT THE PARTIES CAN PROVIDE FURTHER INFORMATION CONCERNING PLAINTIFF'S CHECKS AND EXPENDITURES NOTED IN DEFENDANT'S RECORDS. PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RETAINED IN COURT FILE. COMMISSIONER PAUL SLAVIT. CLERK: S. VANTREE. NOT REPORTED. (506).

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF PAGE, HARRY IS NOT OWED ANY MONEY BY DEFENDANT MOHSIN, SAID AHMED DBA TENDERLOIN LIQUOR ALTHOUGH THE COURT HAS CONCERNS ABOUT THE COMPETANCE OF PLAINTIFF TO AGREE TO THE FINANCIAL ARRANGEMENT WITH DEFENDANT, THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED WAS NOT SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH HIS CLAIM FOR DAMAGES. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
EXHIBITS FROM EXHIBIT REPORT DATED APR-01-2009 ORDERED DESTROYED
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JAN-05-2009 - JUDGMENT.
